scenario: Within Stellar Bank's onboarding form, a Camunda 8.8 Select dropdown is bound to the variable currencyCode and offers seven static options (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, CAD, CHF) for a daily volume of 4500 customers per day. The upstream process initialises currencyCode to MXN, which is not one of the configured options, before the form renders for the customer in Tasklist.
question: How does the Camunda 8.8 Select runtime treat the currencyCode value MXN when it renders the form for the customer?

options.text: It ignores the unmatched data because MXN does not correlate with any option from the configured options source, so the Select renders without any entry pre-selected
It auto-adds MXN as a new dynamic option on first render so the customer can keep the original currency without the modeler needing to update the options source manually
It rejects the form render and surfaces a validation error so the customer cannot continue until the upstream variable is corrected to one of the known options at runtime
It strips the variable from the task scope and rebinds currencyCode to the first option in the source list so the customer always sees a deterministic default selection

a.text: Correct. The Datatypes section states that the Select correlates the data value with the appropriate option defined in the options source and that if no option is found the data is simply ignored, leaving the dropdown without a pre-selection.

b.text: Incorrect. The Select does not augment the options source dynamically with unknown values; the unmatched value is silently ignored rather than appearing as a new option in the dropdown.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented behaviour for an unmatched value is to ignore it, not to block form rendering or surface a validation error at render time.

d.text: Incorrect. The runtime does not modify the bound variable or auto-rebind it to another option; it simply ignores the unmatched value when correlating against the options source.

explanation: Select correlates the bound variable with the values exposed by the options source. When no matching option exists, the data is silently ignored and the dropdown renders without a pre-selected entry, leaving the customer free to pick a valid choice.
